title: Algebra
blurb: Equations, inequalities, systems, exponents — the moves behind a third of any exam.

forms.title: Two-step linear equations
forms.skill: Undo addition, then undo multiplication — in that order.

variants.explanation: Undo the addition first: $2x = 4$, then divide by $2$: $x = 2$. Check: $2\cdot 2 - 2 = 2$.
Undo the addition first: $2x = 6$, then divide by $2$: $x = 3$. Check: $2\cdot 3 + 8 = 14$.
Undo the addition first: $2x = 8$, then divide by $2$: $x = 4$. Check: $2\cdot 4 - 6 = 2$.
Undo the addition first: $2x = 10$, then divide by $2$: $x = 5$. Check: $2\cdot 5 + 4 = 14$.
Undo the addition first: $2x = -4$, then divide by $2$: $x = -2$. Check: $2\cdot -2 - 2 = -6$.
Undo the addition first: $2x = -6$, then divide by $2$: $x = -3$. Check: $2\cdot -3 + 8 = 2$.
Undo the addition first: $2x = 12$, then divide by $2$: $x = 6$. Check: $2\cdot 6 - 3 = 9$.
Undo the addition first: $2x = -8$, then divide by $2$: $x = -4$. Check: $2\cdot -4 - 6 = -14$.
Undo the addition first: $2x = 14$, then divide by $2$: $x = 7$. Check: $2\cdot 7 + 9 = 23$.
Undo the addition first: $2x = -10$, then divide by $2$: $x = -5$. Check: $2\cdot -5 + 4 = -6$.
Undo the addition first: $2x = 16$, then divide by $2$: $x = 8$. Check: $2\cdot 8 - 5 = 11$.
Undo the addition first: $2x = -12$, then divide by $2$: $x = -6$. Check: $2\cdot -6 - 3 = -15$.
Undo the addition first: $3x = 6$, then divide by $3$: $x = 2$. Check: $3\cdot 2 + 8 = 14$.
Undo the addition first: $3x = 9$, then divide by $3$: $x = 3$. Check: $3\cdot 3 - 6 = 3$.
Undo the addition first: $3x = 12$, then divide by $3$: $x = 4$. Check: $3\cdot 4 + 4 = 16$.
Undo the addition first: $3x = 15$, then divide by $3$: $x = 5$. Check: $3\cdot 5 - 3 = 12$.
Undo the addition first: $3x = -6$, then divide by $3$: $x = -2$. Check: $3\cdot -2 + 8 = 2$.
Undo the addition first: $3x = -9$, then divide by $3$: $x = -3$. Check: $3\cdot -3 - 6 = -15$.
Undo the addition first: $3x = 18$, then divide by $3$: $x = 6$. Check: $3\cdot 6 + 9 = 27$.
Undo the addition first: $3x = -12$, then divide by $3$: $x = -4$. Check: $3\cdot -4 + 4 = -8$.
Undo the addition first: $3x = 21$, then divide by $3$: $x = 7$. Check: $3\cdot 7 - 5 = 16$.
Undo the addition first: $3x = -15$, then divide by $3$: $x = -5$. Check: $3\cdot -5 - 3 = -18$.
Undo the addition first: $3x = 24$, then divide by $3$: $x = 8$. Check: $3\cdot 8 + 6 = 30$.
Undo the addition first: $3x = -18$, then divide by $3$: $x = -6$. Check: $3\cdot -6 + 9 = -9$.
Undo the addition first: $4x = 8$, then divide by $4$: $x = 2$. Check: $4\cdot 2 - 6 = 2$.
Undo the addition first: $4x = 12$, then divide by $4$: $x = 3$. Check: $4\cdot 3 + 4 = 16$.
Undo the addition first: $4x = 16$, then divide by $4$: $x = 4$. Check: $4\cdot 4 - 3 = 13$.
Undo the addition first: $4x = 20$, then divide by $4$: $x = 5$. Check: $4\cdot 5 + 9 = 29$.
Undo the addition first: $4x = -8$, then divide by $4$: $x = -2$. Check: $4\cdot -2 - 6 = -14$.
Undo the addition first: $4x = -12$, then divide by $4$: $x = -3$. Check: $4\cdot -3 + 4 = -8$.
Undo the addition first: $4x = 24$, then divide by $4$: $x = 6$. Check: $4\cdot 6 - 5 = 19$.
Undo the addition first: $4x = -16$, then divide by $4$: $x = -4$. Check: $4\cdot -4 - 3 = -19$.
Undo the addition first: $4x = 28$, then divide by $4$: $x = 7$. Check: $4\cdot 7 + 6 = 34$.
Undo the addition first: $4x = -20$, then divide by $4$: $x = -5$. Check: $4\cdot -5 + 9 = -11$.
Undo the addition first: $4x = 32$, then divide by $4$: $x = 8$. Check: $4\cdot 8 + 3 = 35$.
Undo the addition first: $4x = -24$, then divide by $4$: $x = -6$. Check: $4\cdot -6 - 5 = -29$.

Square first, multiply second: $2\cdot 2^2 = 8$, then subtract $6\cdot 6 = 36$: answer $-28$. The square belongs to $a$ alone, not to $2a$.
Square first, multiply second: $2\cdot 2^2 = 8$, then subtract $3\cdot 7 = 21$: answer $-13$. The square belongs to $a$ alone, not to $2a$.
Square first, multiply second: $2\cdot 2^2 = 8$, then subtract $5\cdot 2 = 10$: answer $-2$. The square belongs to $a$ alone, not to $2a$.

forms.title: Inequalities with a sign flip
forms.skill: Dividing by a negative reverses the inequality.

variants.explanation: Subtract $11$: $-2x < -6$. Dividing by $-2$ FLIPS the sign: $x > 3$.
Subtract $-1$: $-2x > 6$. Dividing by $-2$ FLIPS the sign: $x < -3$.
Subtract $10$: $-2x < -8$. Dividing by $-2$ FLIPS the sign: $x > 4$.
Subtract $-6$: $-2x > 8$. Dividing by $-2$ FLIPS the sign: $x < -4$.
Subtract $13$: $-2x < -10$. Dividing by $-2$ FLIPS the sign: $x > 5$.
Subtract $-7$: $-2x > 10$. Dividing by $-2$ FLIPS the sign: $x < -5$.
Subtract $8$: $-2x < -4$. Dividing by $-2$ FLIPS the sign: $x > 2$.
Subtract $0$: $-2x > 4$. Dividing by $-2$ FLIPS the sign: $x < -2$.
Subtract $16$: $-2x < -12$. Dividing by $-2$ FLIPS the sign: $x > 6$.
Subtract $-8$: $-2x > 12$. Dividing by $-2$ FLIPS the sign: $x < -6$.
Subtract $19$: $-2x < -14$. Dividing by $-2$ FLIPS the sign: $x > 7$.
Subtract $-9$: $-2x > 14$. Dividing by $-2$ FLIPS the sign: $x < -7$.
Subtract $14$: $-3x < -9$. Dividing by $-3$ FLIPS the sign: $x > 3$.
Subtract $-4$: $-3x > 9$. Dividing by $-3$ FLIPS the sign: $x < -3$.
Subtract $14$: $-3x < -12$. Dividing by $-3$ FLIPS the sign: $x > 4$.
Subtract $-10$: $-3x > 12$. Dividing by $-3$ FLIPS the sign: $x < -4$.
Subtract $18$: $-3x < -15$. Dividing by $-3$ FLIPS the sign: $x > 5$.
Subtract $-12$: $-3x > 15$. Dividing by $-3$ FLIPS the sign: $x < -5$.
Subtract $10$: $-3x < -6$. Dividing by $-3$ FLIPS the sign: $x > 2$.
Subtract $-2$: $-3x > 6$. Dividing by $-3$ FLIPS the sign: $x < -2$.
Subtract $22$: $-3x < -18$. Dividing by $-3$ FLIPS the sign: $x > 6$.
Subtract $-14$: $-3x > 18$. Dividing by $-3$ FLIPS the sign: $x < -6$.
Subtract $26$: $-3x < -21$. Dividing by $-3$ FLIPS the sign: $x > 7$.
Subtract $-16$: $-3x > 21$. Dividing by $-3$ FLIPS the sign: $x < -7$.

forms.title: Systems of two equations
forms.skill: Eliminate one variable; always check the pair in BOTH equations.

variants.statement: The system $\begin{cases} x + y = 5 \\ 2x - y = 4 \end{cases}$ has solution $(x; y)$. Find $x + y$.
variants.explanation: Eliminate one variable to get $x = 3$, $y = 2$ (check BOTH equations!), so $x + y = 5$.

variants.statement: The system $\begin{cases} x + y = 5 \\ 2x - y = 7 \end{cases}$ has solution $(x; y)$. Find $x + y$.
variants.explanation: Eliminate one variable to get $x = 4$, $y = 1$ (check BOTH equations!), so $x + y = 5$.

variants.statement: The system $\begin{cases} x + y = 7 \\ 2x - y = -1 \end{cases}$ has solution $(x; y)$. Find $x + y$.
variants.explanation: Eliminate one variable to get $x = 2$, $y = 5$ (check BOTH equations!), so $x + y = 7$.

variants.statement: The system $\begin{cases} x + y = 8 \\ 2x - y = 7 \end{cases}$ has solution $(x; y)$. Find $x + y$.
variants.explanation: Eliminate one variable to get $x = 5$, $y = 3$ (check BOTH equations!), so $x + y = 8$.

variants.statement: The system $\begin{cases} x + y = 5 \\ 2x - y = -2 \end{cases}$ has solution $(x; y)$. Find $x + y$.
variants.explanation: Eliminate one variable to get $x = 1$, $y = 4$ (check BOTH equations!), so $x + y = 5$.

variants.statement: The system $\begin{cases} x + y = 8 \\ 2x - y = 10 \end{cases}$ has solution $(x; y)$. Find $x + y$.
variants.explanation: Eliminate one variable to get $x = 6$, $y = 2$ (check BOTH equations!), so $x + y = 8$.

variants.statement: The system $\begin{cases} x + y = 7 \\ 2x - y = 2 \end{cases}$ has solution $(x; y)$. Find $x + y$.
variants.explanation: Eliminate one variable to get $x = 3$, $y = 4$ (check BOTH equations!), so $x + y = 7$.

variants.statement: The system $\begin{cases} x + y = 3 \\ 2x - y = 3 \end{cases}$ has solution $(x; y)$. Find $x + y$.
variants.explanation: Eliminate one variable to get $x = 2$, $y = 1$ (check BOTH equations!), so $x + y = 3$.

variants.statement: The system $\begin{cases} x + y = 5 \\ x - y = 1 \end{cases}$ has solution $(x; y)$. Find $x + y$.
variants.explanation: Eliminate one variable to get $x = 3$, $y = 2$ (check BOTH equations!), so $x + y = 5$.

variants.statement: The system $\begin{cases} x + y = 5 \\ x - y = 3 \end{cases}$ has solution $(x; y)$. Find $x + y$.
variants.explanation: Eliminate one variable to get $x = 4$, $y = 1$ (check BOTH equations!), so $x + y = 5$.

variants.statement: The system $\begin{cases} x + y = 7 \\ x - y = -3 \end{cases}$ has solution $(x; y)$. Find $x + y$.
variants.explanation: Eliminate one variable to get $x = 2$, $y = 5$ (check BOTH equations!), so $x + y = 7$.

variants.statement: The system $\begin{cases} x + y = 8 \\ x - y = 2 \end{cases}$ has solution $(x; y)$. Find $x + y$.
variants.explanation: Eliminate one variable to get $x = 5$, $y = 3$ (check BOTH equations!), so $x + y = 8$.

variants.statement: The system $\begin{cases} x + y = 5 \\ x - y = -3 \end{cases}$ has solution $(x; y)$. Find $x + y$.
variants.explanation: Eliminate one variable to get $x = 1$, $y = 4$ (check BOTH equations!), so $x + y = 5$.

variants.statement: The system $\begin{cases} x + y = 8 \\ x - y = 4 \end{cases}$ has solution $(x; y)$. Find $x + y$.
variants.explanation: Eliminate one variable to get $x = 6$, $y = 2$ (check BOTH equations!), so $x + y = 8$.

variants.statement: The system $\begin{cases} x + y = 7 \\ x - y = -1 \end{cases}$ has solution $(x; y)$. Find $x + y$.
variants.explanation: Eliminate one variable to get $x = 3$, $y = 4$ (check BOTH equations!), so $x + y = 7$.

variants.statement: The system $\begin{cases} x + y = 3 \\ x - y = 1 \end{cases}$ has solution $(x; y)$. Find $x + y$.
variants.explanation: Eliminate one variable to get $x = 2$, $y = 1$ (check BOTH equations!), so $x + y = 3$.

variants.statement: The system $\begin{cases} 2x + y = 8 \\ x - y = 1 \end{cases}$ has solution $(x; y)$. Find $x + y$.
variants.explanation: Eliminate one variable to get $x = 3$, $y = 2$ (check BOTH equations!), so $x + y = 5$.

variants.statement: The system $\begin{cases} 2x + y = 9 \\ x - y = 3 \end{cases}$ has solution $(x; y)$. Find $x + y$.
variants.explanation: Eliminate one variable to get $x = 4$, $y = 1$ (check BOTH equations!), so $x + y = 5$.

variants.statement: The system $\begin{cases} 2x + y = 9 \\ x - y = -3 \end{cases}$ has solution $(x; y)$. Find $x + y$.
variants.explanation: Eliminate one variable to get $x = 2$, $y = 5$ (check BOTH equations!), so $x + y = 7$.

variants.statement: The system $\begin{cases} 2x + y = 13 \\ x - y = 2 \end{cases}$ has solution $(x; y)$. Find $x + y$.
variants.explanation: Eliminate one variable to get $x = 5$, $y = 3$ (check BOTH equations!), so $x + y = 8$.

variants.statement: The system $\begin{cases} 2x + y = 6 \\ x - y = -3 \end{cases}$ has solution $(x; y)$. Find $x + y$.
variants.explanation: Eliminate one variable to get $x = 1$, $y = 4$ (check BOTH equations!), so $x + y = 5$.

variants.statement: The system $\begin{cases} 2x + y = 14 \\ x - y = 4 \end{cases}$ has solution $(x; y)$. Find $x + y$.
variants.explanation: Eliminate one variable to get $x = 6$, $y = 2$ (check BOTH equations!), so $x + y = 8$.

variants.statement: The system $\begin{cases} 2x + y = 10 \\ x - y = -1 \end{cases}$ has solution $(x; y)$. Find $x + y$.
variants.explanation: Eliminate one variable to get $x = 3$, $y = 4$ (check BOTH equations!), so $x + y = 7$.

variants.statement: The system $\begin{cases} 2x + y = 5 \\ x - y = 1 \end{cases}$ has solution $(x; y)$. Find $x + y$.
variants.explanation: Eliminate one variable to get $x = 2$, $y = 1$ (check BOTH equations!), so $x + y = 3$.

variants.statement: The system $\begin{cases} x + 2y = 7 \\ x - y = 1 \end{cases}$ has solution $(x; y)$. Find $x + y$.
variants.explanation: Eliminate one variable to get $x = 3$, $y = 2$ (check BOTH equations!), so $x + y = 5$.

variants.statement: The system $\begin{cases} x + 2y = 6 \\ x - y = 3 \end{cases}$ has solution $(x; y)$. Find $x + y$.
variants.explanation: Eliminate one variable to get $x = 4$, $y = 1$ (check BOTH equations!), so $x + y = 5$.

variants.statement: The system $\begin{cases} x + 2y = 12 \\ x - y = -3 \end{cases}$ has solution $(x; y)$. Find $x + y$.
variants.explanation: Eliminate one variable to get $x = 2$, $y = 5$ (check BOTH equations!), so $x + y = 7$.

variants.statement: The system $\begin{cases} x + 2y = 11 \\ x - y = 2 \end{cases}$ has solution $(x; y)$. Find $x + y$.
variants.explanation: Eliminate one variable to get $x = 5$, $y = 3$ (check BOTH equations!), so $x + y = 8$.

variants.statement: The system $\begin{cases} x + 2y = 9 \\ x - y = -3 \end{cases}$ has solution $(x; y)$. Find $x + y$.
variants.explanation: Eliminate one variable to get $x = 1$, $y = 4$ (check BOTH equations!), so $x + y = 5$.

variants.statement: The system $\begin{cases} x + 2y = 10 \\ x - y = 4 \end{cases}$ has solution $(x; y)$. Find $x + y$.
variants.explanation: Eliminate one variable to get $x = 6$, $y = 2$ (check BOTH equations!), so $x + y = 8$.

variants.statement: The system $\begin{cases} x + 2y = 11 \\ x - y = -1 \end{cases}$ has solution $(x; y)$. Find $x + y$.
variants.explanation: Eliminate one variable to get $x = 3$, $y = 4$ (check BOTH equations!), so $x + y = 7$.

variants.statement: The system $\begin{cases} x + 2y = 4 \\ x - y = 1 \end{cases}$ has solution $(x; y)$. Find $x + y$.
variants.explanation: Eliminate one variable to get $x = 2$, $y = 1$ (check BOTH equations!), so $x + y = 3$.

variants.statement: The system $\begin{cases} x - y = 1 \\ 2x + y = 8 \end{cases}$ has solution $(x; y)$. Find $x + y$.

variants.explanation: Eliminate one variable to get $x = 5$, $y = 3$ (check BOTH equations!), so $x + y = 8$.

forms.title: Rational equations
forms.skill: Clear the denominator, solve, then verify the denominator isn't zero.

variants.explanation: Multiply both sides by $x + 2$ (allowed since $x \ne -2$): $x + 4 = 2(x + 2)$, which gives $x = 0$. The denominator is nonzero there, so it is valid.

forms.title: Absolute-value inequalities
forms.skill: |x − a| ≤ b is a distance statement: a ± b bracket the answer.

variants.explanation: $|x - 3| \le 2$ is a distance statement: $3 - 2 \le x \le 3 + 2$, i.e. $[1;\, 5]$. The 'or' split belongs to $\ge$, not $\le$.
$|x - 3| \ge 3$ means the distance from $x$ to $3$ is at least $3$: $x \le 0$ or $x \ge 6$. The union belongs to $\ge$, not a single bracket.
$|x - 3| \le 4$ is a distance statement: $3 - 4 \le x \le 3 + 4$, i.e. $[-1;\, 7]$. The 'or' split belongs to $\ge$, not $\le$.
$|x - 3| \ge 5$ means the distance from $x$ to $3$ is at least $5$: $x \le -2$ or $x \ge 8$. The union belongs to $\ge$, not a single bracket.

forms.title: Systems with a parameter
forms.skill: No solution ⟺ slopes match, intercepts don't (proportional coefficients, unproportional constants).

variants.statement: For which value of $m$ does the system $\begin{cases} x + 2y = 2 \\ 2x + my = 5 \end{cases}$ have NO solution?
variants.explanation: No solution = parallel lines: coefficients proportional but constants not. $\frac{2}{1} = \frac{m}{2}$ gives $m = 4$, and $\frac{5}{2} \ne \frac{2}{1}$ confirms they never meet.

variants.statement: For which value of $m$ does the system $\begin{cases} x + 2y = 3 \\ 2x + my = 7 \end{cases}$ have NO solution?
